subject: Re: M not responding
body:
Hi all,

Just an update: the patched version of Warp/M runs fine without any
crashes.
Thank you all for chipping in suggestions.
Cheers,
Shyamal


On Wed, Aug 19, 2020 at 12:02 PM Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Shyamal,
>
> that's exactly the dreaded "particle outside of image boundaries" error I
> mentioned. Hope the update helps!
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Wednesday, August 19, 2020 at 8:18:51 AM UTC+2, shyamal mosalaganti
> wrote:
>>
>> Hi Alister, Dimitry & others,
>>
>> So based on your suggestions, i re-ran M with changing permissions. It
>> ran through most of my data ~480/570 tilt series before finally crashing
>> with the following error (see below).
>> I will re-run it using the patched version which Dimitry recommended.
>> Nevertheless, any ideas why this happened?
>> Thanks,
>> Shyamal
>> [image: Screenshot 2020-08-19 at 08.14.22.png]
>>
>> On Tue, Aug 18, 2020 at 6:01 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Shyamal,
>>>
>>> I have a weird recurring issue which is similar, M sometimes hangs when
>>> trying to write files at the end of a refinement. In my case it seems to be
>>> some file permissions issue, even though everywhere I have full access
>>> unclicking the read-only checkbox and applying recursively down through the
>>> directory seems to fix it!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Alister
>>>
>>> On 18 Aug 2020, at 17:41, shyamal narayan <narayanshyamal@gmail.com>
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>> ﻿
>>> Hi Dimitry and others,
>>>
>>> I am running M (v1.0.9) and there was no problem. However, after
>>> sometime there is no progress and it stuck on a tilt series (TS_749, please
>>> see attached) for almost an hour now.
>>> Did anyone have a similar problem? and is there a way to fix this?
>>> Please let me know,
>>> Thank you,
>>> Shyamal
>>> <Screenshot 2020-08-18 at 17.34.59.png>
>>>
>>> --
